Class MaskedView

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
com.dlsc.gemsfx.MaskedView
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class MaskedView extends javafx.scene.control.Control
A view that takes a content node and applies advanced clipping so that its left and right side will "fade out". This is especially useful in combination with controls that want to show scrolling controls on both sides for moving things to the left and right as those controls will then be nicely visible. The StripView control is using the masked view inside its skin.

CSS Styleable Properties:

CSS Properties
PropertyTypeDescription
-fx-fading-sizedoubleSize of the side fading areas in pixels

  • Property Details

    • content

      public final javafx.beans.property.SimpleObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> contentProperty
      The content that will be shown inside the masked view.
      See Also:
      • getContent()
      • setContent(Node)
      • contentProperty()

    • fadingSize

      public final javafx.beans.property.DoubleProperty fadingSizeProperty
      The width of the clips on the left and right hand side of the view. This property defines how big the fade in / out areas will be.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-fading-size property. Valid values are: positive numbers. The default value is 120.

      See Also:
      • getFadingSize()
      • setFadingSize(double)
      • fadingSizeProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • MaskedView

      public MaskedView()
      Constructs a new masked view. Content can be specified later by calling setContent(Node).

    • MaskedView

      public MaskedView(javafx.scene.Node content)
      Constructs a new masked view for the given content.

    • getFadingSize

      public final double getFadingSize()
      Gets the value of the fadingSize property.
      Property description:
      The width of the clips on the left and right hand side of the view. This property defines how big the fade in / out areas will be.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-fading-size property. Valid values are: positive numbers. The default value is 120.

      Returns:
      the value of the fadingSize property
      See Also:
      • setFadingSize(double)
      • fadingSizeProperty()

    • fadingSizeProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.DoubleProperty fadingSizeProperty()
      The width of the clips on the left and right hand side of the view. This property defines how big the fade in / out areas will be.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-fading-size property. Valid values are: positive numbers. The default value is 120.

      Returns:
      the size of the side fading areas / clip areas
      See Also:
      • getFadingSize()
      • setFadingSize(double)
